title: Build Your First AI Agent as a Finance Professional
description: Go from zero to your first working AI automation — taking a repetitive weekly finance task and having AI handle it for you.

steps.title: Identify your most repetitive weekly task
steps.content: Pick the one you dread. Usually it's copying data from one system and summarizing it in another (e.g., pulling GL data into a variance report, consolidating budget inputs).

steps.title: Open Claude and describe the task in plain English
steps.content: Don't worry about "prompting perfectly." Just explain what you do, what the inputs look like, and what the output should be. Ask Claude to help you automate it.

steps.title: Test and iterate until it works at 70%+
steps.content: Your first attempt won't be perfect. Refine the instructions, add examples of what good output looks like, and correct mistakes. If it gets to 70% accuracy, you have a working foundation.

steps.title: Try Claude inside Excel for spreadsheet tasks
steps.content: If your repetitive task involves spreadsheets (most finance tasks do), install the Claude Excel add-in and use it to clean data, write formulas, or audit models directly in your workbook.

steps.title: Connect Claude to PowerPoint for reporting
steps.content: Take the outputs from your analysis and ask Claude to pull key findings into a board deck or management report. Same conversation, minutes instead of hours.

steps.title: Evaluate AI-native finance tools
steps.content: Look at whether purpose-built AI finance tools (for FP&A, close management, reconciliation) could replace parts of your legacy stack. Compare the ROI.

steps.title: Audit your existing software for AI features
steps.content: Check whether tools you already pay for (your ERP, BI platform, expense management) have shipped AI features you haven't turned on yet.
